evidence_quotes.quote: Advertisement abuse; for context, today a normal user can't view a 10-minute video on YouTube without being faced with multiple advertisements regardless if it's non-copyright content; in comparison to the early 2000s having advertisement side banners on websites weren't half as abusive as the current status is.

Lack of transparency; there have been cases of ToS infringement warnings without evidence towards users and when inquired passed as the users broke ToS, when most oftenly they just re-post something. Some platforms will also mention they are against software assisted exploitative behaviors though once these become to frequent and disguised in the sea of users and bots they will pretend to moderate said behaviors but in truth ignore them if these have no massive visibility. An example of this would be in rankings if the abuser is in the top 10 the account will be punished, but if the abuser is outside the public leaderboards they will go unpunished. (this logic applies to every place in the internet like social networks and more).

Influencers abuse; these seem to have become to common practice in several areas from suggestive content to from including advertisements sections in their content without adequate identifiers, active scams and misleading presentations of products.

Promotion of addictive practices, it's well known in the online community, example: casino game streamers, often some of these have been found promoting gambling, following script behavior in non legalized and law confirming casino games to show the game has better odds than it actually has, etc...

Price manipulation; well these are often spotted by those that understand the scenario, but what i most oftenly confirm is that some consumers are usually clueless to these manipulations and end up feeding and justify it's practice.

Selling trashware; this is an issue that partially gets overlooked due to returns that normally the store ends up solving, though the issue stems from distributors and even creators due to them creating amounts of keys that went unsold and continue to sell these when they no longer work or never worked correctly in the current operating systems.

AI suggestions; in areas where consumers should have diversity of content modern AI suggestions seem to not preserve a decent selection of content diversity. Compromising equality standards of opportunity for content creators as well as limiting the consumer options.

Inefficient Customer support; personally i have faced situations where a concern or an issue arose and since the implementation of sorting tickets through pre-made menus and bots oftenly don't have the option to address the concerning matter (due to it being an issue out of the predicted norm). Leading to sometimes reports in the inadequate section and leading to it being ignored since the ticket will not be redirected to the correct section.(an analogy, would be a consumer going to a restaurant be faced with an inedible meal, report to the waiter to take it back and the waiter ignores the appeal do nothing because the customer should've complained to the chef, while customer having no way of knowing if the complaint went through or not)

paragraphs.text: There are some key-points in the Digital Fairness Act, that truly need to be analyzed thoroughly and call for more rigorous action against. - Advertisement abuse; for context, today a normal user can't view a 10-minute video on YouTube without being faced with multiple advertisements regardless if it's non-copyright content; in comparison to the early 2000s having advertisement side banners on websites weren't half as abusive as the current status is. - Influencers abuse; these seem to have become to common practice in several areas from suggestive content to from including advertisements sections in their content without adequate identifiers, active scams and misleading presentations of products. - Promotion of addictive practices, it's well known in the online community, example: casino game streamers, often some of these have been found promoting gambling, following script behavior in non legalized and law confirming casino games to show the game has better odds than it actually has, etc... - Price manipulation; well these are often spotted by those that understand the scenario, but what i most oftenly confirm is that some consumers are usually clueless to these manipulations and end up feeding and justify it's practice. - Inefficient Customer support; personally i have faced situations where a concern or an issue arose and since the implementation of sorting tickets through pre-made menus and bots oftenly don't have the option to address the concerning matter (due to it being an issue out of the predicted norm). Leading to sometimes reports in the inadequate section and leading to it being ignored since the ticket will not be redirected to the correct section.(an analogy, would be a consumer going to a restaurant be faced with an inedible meal, report to the waiter to take it back and the waiter ignores the appeal do nothing because the customer should've complained to the chef, while customer having no way of knowing if the complaint went through or not) - Lack of transparency; there have been cases of ToS infringement warnings without evidence towards users and when inquired passed as the users broke ToS, when most oftenly they just re-post something. Some platforms will also mention they are against software assisted exploitative behaviors though once these become to frequent and disguised in the sea of users and bots they will pretend to moderate said behaviors but in truth ignore them if these have no massive visibility. An example of this would be in rankings if the abuser is in the top 10 the account will be punished, but if the abuser is outside the public leaderboards they will go unpunished. (this logic applies to every place in the internet like social networks and more). - Selling trashware; this is an issue that partially gets overlooked due to returns that normally the store ends up solving, though the issue stems from distributors and even creators due to them creating amounts of keys that went unsold and continue to sell these when they no longer work or never worked correctly in the current operating systems. - AI suggestions; in areas where consumers should have diversity of content modern AI suggestions seem to not preserve a decent selection of content diversity. Compromising equality standards of opportunity for content creators as well as limiting the consumer options. Hope that through this document more pressure can be made on the main platforms allowing these malpractices and to create if not preserve jobs in these industries.
